title: Interest rates and the term structure

questions.prompt: A credit card has an APR of 18% with monthly compounding. What is the EAR? Answer as a percent to two decimals.
Nominal interest rate i = 6%, inflation π = 3%. Using the approximate Fisher equation r ≈ i − π, what is the approximate real rate? Answer as a percent.
Why has yield-curve inversion historically been associated with recession?
choices: It typically reflects Fed tightening pushing short rates up while markets expect lower long-run growth and inflation
It reflects an SEC rule change
questions.explanation: An inverted curve is a marker, not a cause: aggressive Fed tightening at the short end + lower long-run growth/inflation expectations at the long end together produce the shape, and both forces tend to precede recessions.

questions.prompt: If 2022 had nominal Treasury yields around 4% and US inflation near 9%, what was approximately true of real returns on cash?
questions.explanation: r ≈ i − π = 4% − 9% = −5%. Cash savers lost purchasing power even with positive nominal yields.

furtherReading.title: The most well-known recession indicator stopped flashing red, but now another one is going off
furtherReading.why: Covers the historic 10y-2y inversion that ran Oct 2022 through Dec 2024 — the longest yield-curve inversion in 45 years — and its real-time market interpretation.
